title: Main questions
text: Research

title: Research
text:
Metatranscriptomics
We develop approaches to characterize transcriptomes from complex environmental and biological samples.

Key Questions

Which organisms contribute RNA to a sample?
How can transcriptional activity be measured accurately?
What unexplored RNA species exist?

title: Research
text:
RNA Viruses
RNA viruses represent one of the largest reservoirs of unexplored biological diversity.

Current Topics

Virus discovery
Viral evolution
Host-virus interactions

title: Research
text:
Circular RNAs
Circular RNAs remain poorly understood in many biological contexts.

Current Topics

Biogenesis
Functional characterization
Biomarker development

title: Research
text:
Computational Methods
We build computational pipelines for large-scale transcriptomic analysis.

Focus Areas

Assembly
Annotation
Classification
Machine learning

title: About
text: About this site

title: Mutz Lab
text:
Our research investigates the diversity, function, and evolution of RNA molecules in biological systems, with a particular focus on metatranscriptomics, virology, and emerging RNA species.


We aim to develop experimental and computational approaches that reveal hidden layers of transcriptional complexity across cells, microbes, viruses, and circular RNAs.




Metatranscriptomics
RNA virus discovery
Circular RNAs
Computational RNA biology
Host-pathogen interactions





New publication on RNA diversity (May 2026)
New PhD position available (April 2026)
Presentation at XYZ Conference (March 2026)

Explore our research

title: Virus Evolution
text:
Our research investigates the evolution of viruses and how it impacts the interplay of viruses and their hosts. We focus on RNA viruses and novel RNA replicators with the aim to identify emerging viruses and what restricts cross-species transmission.


Our research aims to increase our understanding of virus evolution to improve human health. Therefore, we combine computational and experimental approaches to study virus evolution and virus-host inetractions at scale: From microscopic virus-host protein interactions to the population level.




RNA virus discovery
Characterization of novel, replicating circular RNAs
Host-pathogen interactions





Emerging viruses
virology at scale





Pascal Mutz will start his independent group at the University of Ostrava within the LERCO project.

Explore our research
